supply_chain.dependencies: Healthcare industry is labor intensive and dependent on nurses, physicians, and clinical staff availability
risks: Nationwide shortage of nurses and clinical staff
Increased demand for care putting strain on resources and staff
Required to utilize higher-cost temporary labor and pay premiums for essential workers
Personnel shortages may require enhanced wages and benefits or limit services
Mandatory nurse-staffing ratios in certain states like California could adversely affect operations
Increased labor union activity could adversely affect labor costs

regulatory_risks: Extensive federal, state and local regulations relating to hospital operations, billing practices, relationships with physicians, patient privacy (HIPAA), emergency medical treatment (EMTALA)
Medicare and Medicaid reimbursement changes and reductions
Anti-kickback statute and Stark Law compliance risks
False Claims Act liability and qui tam whistleblower lawsuits
Certificate of Need (CON) laws in certain states restricting expansion
State conversion legislation affecting acquisition of not-for-profit hospitals
Corporate practice of medicine restrictions in states like Florida, Nevada, California, Texas
Value-based purchasing programs tying reimbursement to quality metrics
Hospital readmission reduction program penalties
Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (ACA) provisions and potential repeal/modification
Medicaid Disproportionate Share Hospital (DSH) payment reductions scheduled to begin in 2025
Price transparency requirements for hospitals effective January 1, 2021
No Surprises Act provisions on surprise billing and independent dispute resolution
State rate review and indigent tax provisions
Medical malpractice tort law and potential reforms
Environmental regulations on medical waste disposal
Red Flags Rule on identity theft prevention
Patient Safety and Quality Improvement Act reporting requirements
Foreign regulations in United Kingdom including registration, licensing, employee regulation, clinical standards, data protection (UK GDPR)
Potential new tariffs on imported pharmaceutical ingredients, medical devices, and medical equipment

sanction_risks: Changes in U.S. trade policies and increased trade restrictions, tariffs or taxes on imports could impact business operations
New tariffs imposed or threatened on imported products from Mexico, Canada and China in February 2025
Significant tariffs on imported pharmaceutical ingredients, medical devices, medical equipment could strain supply chains and increase costs
